title: Deliciously Creamy Tunisian Boudin-Blanc Tagine
description: A rich and creamy Tunisian tagine made with boudin blanc, served in a ttukbaegi earthenware cookware.
instructions: In a large ttukbaegi earthenware cookware, melt the unsalted butter over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until softened, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the boudin blanc and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it is no longer pink, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, heavy cream, plain flour, ground cumin, ground ginger, and salt. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, uncovered, until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together, about 15-20 minutes.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and cilantro.
Season the tagine to taste with additional salt and spices, if needed.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional fresh parsley and cilantro, if desired.
